title: Slow slirp4netns / pasta network performance
body:
### Issue Description

I am getting on a fresh Fedora CoreOS ( EC2 ) instance ( us-east, nothing is installed, except pasta, via rpm-ostree ) slow slirp4netns / pasta network performance. The instance is quite capable ( Up to 12.5 Gigabit network bandwidth ).
Tried most of the America https://github.com/R0GGER/public-iperf3-servers#america public iperf servers with +- same results in terms of bridge vs pasta/slirp4.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `(sudo)  podman run --rm -it --net slirp4netns/pasta (podman) alpine sh -c 'apk add iperf3 && iperf3 -c nyfiosspeed4.west.verizon.net'`


### Describe the results you received

iper3 performance result is in MB

### Describe the results you expected

iper3 performance result should be at least in GBs, even knowing the fact that slirp4pnetns and is slower than bridge.

### Podman in a container

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

- AWS EC2 instance ( m6i.4xlarge )
- FCOS 37.20230312.20.0
- pasta 0^20230310.g70c0765-1.fc37.x86_64

comments.body:
For pasta, we're tracking the issue with Verizon iperf3 servers at https://bugs.passt.top/show_bug.cgi?id=44, and I can't reproduce the issue with any public iperf3 server in Europe (with client also located in Europe) -- iperf3 just fills the 1gbps uplink I have available there (~923 mbps).

As for slirp4netns, I can reproduce similar results even with server and client located in Europe: there latency drives the throughput, given that libslirp doesn't support TCP window scaling, and something around 200mbps is expected if you have even just 10ms RTT, see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bandwidth-delay_product for details. You don't state the RTT to the iperf3 server, but I think it's the same (expected) "issue" I see.

In any case, this has nothing to do with Podman, and, by the way, filing several reports in seemingly [random places](https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1436#issuecomment-1476251102) doesn't really help solving the issue, because it distracts developers.
